title: This Marblehead man is running the Boston Marathon for his pediatric patients
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Brian Wishart, 42, from Marblehead, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support pediatric patients at Spaulding Rehabilitation.
Wishart leads the Pediatric Rehabilitation Medicine division, focusing on integrating research and improving care for children.
Funds raised will aid adaptive sports programs and enhance resources for child life specialists at Spaulding Rehab.

title: 8 children have now died from flu in Mass. this season
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Eight children have died from the flu this season in Massachusetts, according to state health data.
As of February 28, 298 adults and eight children have died from influenza in Massachusetts.
Flu severity was low in Boston during the last week of February, but experts still urge vaccination.

title: NBA cancels Hawks’ ‘Magic City’ promotion amid backlash following Luke Kornet’s letter
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: NBA cancels Atlanta Hawks' 'Magic City Monday' promotion after backlash from stakeholders and a letter by Luke Kornet.
The promotion aimed to celebrate Atlanta's Magic City strip club with themed wings, merchandise, and a performance by rapper T.I.
Luke Kornet's viral letter raised concerns about promoting a strip club at family-friendly NBA games, gaining support from some players.

title: Shaquille O’Neal had no clue who Celtics guard Baylor Scheierman is
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Shaquille O'Neal was unaware of Celtics guard Baylor Scheierman despite his prominent role during Jayson Tatum's absence.
Baylor Scheierman has played 59 games, averaging 4.8 points and 3.2 rebounds per game for the Celtics this season.
Scheierman's minutes remain consistent even with Jayson Tatum's return, reflecting his valuable contribution to the team.

title: Healey calls on aviation company operating at Hanscom to cut ties with ICE
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Gov. Maura Healey urges Signature Aviation to cut ties with ICE over deportation flights at Hanscom Field.
Signature Aviation, based in Florida, is the only fixed base operator serving ICE at Hanscom Field, Bedford.
ICE conducted 132 flights from Hanscom in Trump's first year, with 13 flights in January 2026, according to data.

title: Delivery person falls through a shaft outside Boston’s Omni Parker House
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: A delivery worker fell 20 feet after a metal grate gave way at Boston's Omni Parker House.
The Boston Fire Department rescued the conscious worker, who was then hospitalized for treatment.
Boston Inspectional Services cited the hotel for a building code violation due to rotted brackets.

title: Quincy gas station looking for violist to ‘serenade’ customers
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Quincy Gas owner Harvey Kertzman seeks violist to perform at his gas station located on Quincy Avenue.
Kertzman has found a retired violist willing to perform once the weather is warm enough.
Signs at the gas station continue to seek additional violists as backup performers.

title: Report: Patriots agree to sign edge rusher Dre’Mont Jones
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots signing edge rusher Dre'Mont Jones to a three-year deal worth up to $39.5 million.
Jones, previously with Baltimore, recorded a career-high seven sacks and 43 tackles last season.
The Patriots ranked 26th in sacks in 2025; Jones led his previous team in QB hits and pressure rate.

title: How a Boston Marathon runner finds purpose in mentoring and miles
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Victoria Walker is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for Minds Matter Boston, combining her passions for mentoring and running.
Walker mentors two 16-year-olds at Minds Matter Boston, helping students from low-income backgrounds with the college application process.
Running has provided Walker with a community and purpose, enhancing her connection to Boston and supporting her mentees' educational goals.

title: Meet the New England Aquarium’s viral seal and his rubber duckie pal
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Reggae, a harbor seal at New England Aquarium, went viral after playing with a rubber duckie in a video.
The Instagram video amassed nearly 500,000 views, showcasing the seal's intelligence and curiosity, said social media manager Marisa Bernal.
Trainers use cognitive enrichment activities like 'Find It', involving seals locating objects, to stimulate and engage the animals.
